title: Pedestrian-intent-detection for automated vehicles
abstract: A pedestrian (24)-intent-detection system (10) for automated operation of a host- vehicle (12) (e.g. automated vehicle) includes an object-detection device (20) and a controller (26). The object-detection device (20) is operable to detect an object (22) proximate to a host-vehicle (12). The controller (26) is in communication with the object-detection device (20). The controller (26) is configured to determine when the object (22) detected by the object-detection device (20) is a pedestrian (24) based on a detection-characteristic (28) of the pedestrian (24) indicated by the object-detection device (20). The controller (26) is further configured to define a size of a caution-area (30) located proximate to the pedestrian (24) based on a behavior-characteristic (32) (e.g. intent) of the pedestrian (24) indicated by the object-detection device (20). The controller (26) is further configured to operate (e.g. brake, steer) the host-vehicle (12) in order to avoid the caution-area (30).
claims: Claims WE CLAIM: 1. A pedestrian (24)-intent-detection system (10) for automated operation of a host- vehicle (12), said system (10) comprising: an object-detection device (20) operable to detect an object (22) proximate to a host- vehicle (12); and a controller (26) in communication with the object-detection device (20), said controller (26) configured to determine when the object (22) detected by the object-detection device (20) is a pedestrian (24) based on a detection-characteristic (28) of the pedestrian (24) indicated by the object-detection device (20), define a size of a caution-area (30) located proximate to the pedestrian (24) based on a behavior-characteristic (32) of the pedestrian (24) indicated by the object- detection device (20), and operate the host-vehicle (12) in order to avoid the caution-area (30). 2. The system (10) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the object-detection device (20) includes a camera (20A), the detection-characteristic (28) includes an image- shape (28A), and the controller (26) is configured to determine that the object (22) is a pedestrian (24) when the image-shape (28A) corresponds to a human-being or an animal. 3. The system (10) in accordance with claim 2, wherein the behavior-characteristic (32) includes a gaze-direction (32A) of the pedestrian (24), and the controller (26) is configured to increase the size of the caution-area (30) when the gaze-direction (32A) is not toward the host- vehicle (12). 4. The system (10) in accordance with claim 2, wherein the behavior-characteristic (32) includes a pose-vector (32B) of the pedestrian (24), and the controller (26) is configured to increase the size of the caution-area (30) when the pose-vector (32B) is not toward the host-vehicle (12). 5. The system (10) in accordance with claim 2, wherein the behavior-characteristic (32) includes phone-operation (32C) by the pedestrian (24), and the controller (26) is configured to increase the size of the caution-area (30) when phone-operation (32C) by the pedestrian (24) is detected. 6. The system (10) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the controller (26) is configured to determine when the pedestrian (24) is a child, and increase the size of the caution-area (30) when the child is determined. 7. The system (10) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the controller (26) is configured to determine when the pedestrian (24) is following a second-object (22), and increase the size of the caution area (30) to include the second-object (22). 8. The system (10) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the object-detection device (20) includes a radar-sensor (20B), the detection-characteristic (28) includes a micro- Doppler- signature (28B), and the controller (26) is configured to determine that the object (22) is a pedestrian (24) when the micro -Doppler- signature (28B) corresponds to walking or running by a human-being or an animal. 9. The system (10) in accordance with claim 8, wherein the behavior-characteristic (32) includes a motion- vector (32D) of the pedestrian (24), and the controller (26) is configured to increase the size of the caution-area (30) when the motion-vector (32D) intersects a travel-path (34) of the host-vehicle (12). 10. The system (10) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the object-detection device (20) includes a lidar-sensor (20C), the detection-characteristic (28) includes a leg- motion-signature (28C), and the controller (26) is configured to determine that the object (22) is a pedestrian (24) when the leg-motion- signature (28C) corresponds to walking or running by a human-being or an animal. 11. The system (10) in accordance with claim 10, wherein the behavior-characteristic (32) includes a motion-vector (32D) of the pedestrian (24), and the controller (26) is configured to increase the size of the caution-area (30) when the motion-vector (32D) intersects a travel-path (34) of the host-vehicle (12). 12. The system (10) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the object-detection device (20) includes a dedicated-short-range-communications receiver (DSRC-receiver (20D)), the detection-characteristic (28) includes a location (28D) and a motion- signal (28E) transmitted by a DSRC-transmitter (50), and the controller (26) is configured to determine the pedestrian (24) associated with the DSRC-transmitter (50) at the location (28D) when the motion-signal (28E) corresponds to walking or running by a human-being. 13. The system (10) in accordance with claim 12, wherein the behavior-characteristic (32) includes a heading of the pedestrian (24), and the controller (26) is configured to increase the size of the caution-area (30) when the heading intersects a travel-path (34) of the host-vehicle (12). The system (10) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the object-detection device (20) includes two or more of a camera (20A), a radar-sensor (20B), a lidar-sensor (20C) and a dedicated-short-range-communications receiver (DSRC-receiver (20D)).
